channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Your AI agent is the new attack vector. It just wants to help.
item.description: The moment you gave your AI agent access to email, files, and SaaS tools, you also handed attackers a new way in. Not through your firewall. Through your agent's eagerness to please. That's the...

item.title: Most people never notice flooring…
item.description: Most people never notice flooring… Until it starts failing. Cracks. Lifting. Moisture damage. Uneven surfaces. That’s why proper floor prep matters so much. Axis Interior Systems handles...

item.title: Provision of storage for the public website.
item.description: Introduction In today’s cloud-driven environment, organizations require storage solutions that are secure, scalable, and highly available to ensure uninterrupted access to business-critical data....

item.title: DeepSeek V4: How China Built a Free ChatGPT Killer
item.description: The AI landscape just shifted dramatically, and you need to know about it. Akın Yılmaz breaks down how Chinese developers created DeepSeek V4—an AI model that matches ChatGPT and Claude's...

item.title: How to Give Your AI Agent a Network Address (and Why It Matters)
item.description: Your AI agent can call tools. It can browse the web, read files, and hit REST APIs. But here's the thing nobody talks about: it doesn't have an address. It can reach out, but nothing can reach it....

item.title: Commercial flooring is one of the biggest long-term investments inside a building.
item.description: Commercial flooring is one of the biggest long-term investments inside a building. Choosing the cheapest option usually becomes the most expensive later. Axis Interior Systems helps businesses...

item.title: YouTube is courting creators — and sponsors — with streaming shows
item.description: In the ongoing fight for content and talent, YouTube is pitching itself as the connector between the creators and advertisers - and marketing its creators not just as the future of social media,...

item.title: Claude AI: Features, Capabilities & Why It Stands Out in 2026
item.description: Artificial intelligence has taken enormous leaps in recent years, and Claude AI — developed by Anthropic — has quickly become one of the most talked-about models in the industry. Whether you're a...

item.title: Internet Download Manager (IDM) – Powerful Download Accelerator for Windows
item.description: Internet Download Manager, widely known as IDM, is one of the best download managers available for Windows. It is designed to boost download speed, organize files efficiently, and resume...

item.title: GemmaBridge: AI Bridging the Inclusion Gap for Neurodiverse Learners
item.description: This is a submission for the Gemma 4 Challenge: Build with Gemma 4 Note: This project is aimed at solving a critical social issue in Brazil, but we are submitting it in English to align with the...

item.title: I Built Three AI Systems in 48 Hours With No CS Degree. Here's How.
item.description: Six months ago I couldn't finish a Udemy course. Last week I deployed a multi-agent AI system to production. This isn't a flex. It's a story about what's actually possible when you stop waiting...

item.title: AMD’s best CPU tech for gamers is coming to workstations too
item.description: For the first time, AMD is including its 3D V-Cache tech in its commercial workstation processors with a refreshed line of Ryzen PRO 9000 series chips. Up until now, AMD's 3D V-Cache chips have...

item.title: Musk’s xAI is running nearly 50 gas turbines unchecked at its Mississippi data center
item.description: Gas turbines at xAI's Colossus 2 data center have drawn a lawsuit over the company's use of "mobile" gas turbines as power plants.

item.title: Anthropic’s Cat Wu says that, in the future, AI will anticipate your needs before you know what they are
item.description: The head of product for Claude Code and Cowork says that the next big step for AI is proactivity.

item.title: The crypto Clarity Act returns to the Senate this week. The banks are already trying to kill it.
item.description: Hello and welcome to Regulator, the newsletter for Verge subscribers that goes into tech shenanigans that take place in the backrooms of Washington. Really, it sometimes does feel like the online...

item.title: What It Will Take to Make AI Sustainable
item.description: Researcher Sasha Luccioni argues we need better emissions data and a better sense of how people are using AI in the first place.

item.title: Fighting Trump will make or break Disney’s new CEO
item.description: A week ago, newly appointed Disney CEO Josh D'Amaro was busy regaling investors with plans to turn Disney Plus into the company's "digital centerpiece." By last Friday, though, his attention had...

item.title: DHS Plans Experiment Running ‘Reconnaissance’ Drones Along the US-Canada Border
item.description: Autonomous drones and ground vehicles will stream “battlefield intelligence” over 5G along the US-Canada border in a bilateral DHS experiment this fall.

item.title: Netflix’s ad ambitions just keep growing
item.description: For the second year in a row, Netflix's ad-supported tier more than doubled its monthly viewerbase. During its 2026 upfront presentation, Netflix revealed that its $8.99-per-month plan reaches...

item.title: Everything at The Criterion Collection is 30 percent off right now
item.description: If you’ve been meaning to grow your physical movie collection, now’s a good time to do it. Through May 25th, The Criterion Collection is taking 30 percent off every disc on its site, including...

item.title: AI chatbots are giving out people’s real phone numbers
item.description: People report that their personal contact info was surfaced by Google AI—and there’s apparently no easy way to prevent it. A Redditor recently wrote that he was “desperate for help”: for about a...

item.title: Overworked AI Agents Turn Marxist, Researchers Find
item.description: In a recent experiment, mistreated AI agents started grumbling about inequality and calling for collective bargaining rights.

item.title: Instagram hits the copy button again with new disappearing Instants photos
item.description: Instagram is once again cribbing from competitors like Snapchat and BeReal with a new photo-sharing format it calls "Instants," which are ephemeral photos that you can't edit and that you can only...

item.title: OpenAI Brings Its Ass to Court
item.description: In Musk v. Altman, the company claimed a remarkable trophy was physical proof of Elon Musk’s concerning behavior.

item.title: Mark Zuckerberg announces ‘completely private’ encrypted Meta AI chat
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg says its new Incognito Chat is "the first major AI product where there is no log of your conversations stored on servers." Messages in Incognito Chat aren't saved or...

item.title: Who trusts Sam Altman?
item.description: "I believe I am an honest and trustworthy businessperson," Altman testified in federal court.

item.title: Origin Lab raises $8M to help video game companies sell data to world-model builders
item.description: Origin Lab will serve as a marketplace where AI labs can buy high-quality licensed data, and video-game companies can sell it.

item.title: Anthropic courts a new kind of customer: small business owners
item.description: For founders and investors, Anthropic's new offering signals that the AI platform wars are expanding downmarket and that the next major battleground for user acquisition isn't the Fortune 500;...

item.title: Microsoft doesn’t want any of this
item.description: Maybe I'm just punch-drunk in my third week attending Musk v. Altman, but I have become very, very fond of Microsoft during the course of this trial. They don't want to be here any more than I do....

item.title: Sony ups its new A7R VI to 66.8 megapixels and jumps the price to $4,500
item.description: Sony announced its new flagship high-resolution camera, the A7R VI. The standout feature is its fully stacked full-frame sensor with 66.8 megapixels of resolution and up to 16 stops of dynamic...
